source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Pryzm

Pryzm is a startup revolutionizing government contracting, reshaping the way that companies and governments work together. Founded by alumni of top Defense and Artificial Intelligence firms (including Palantir and Lockheed Martin), backed by preeminent investors, and deployed with leading defense companies, join us in our mission to enable our government with the best technology and services possible!



Who we are looking for:

You are a self-starter, ready and able to get things done, and relentless in your pursuit of the mission - nothing will stop you. Intelligent, curious, technically strong, collaborative, and driven. Comfortable operating in ambiguous environments. A maniacal sense of urgency is your operating principle.



What you'll do:

Partner closely with our founding team to develop the product used by our government partners to accomplish their goals, such as tracking funds, executing projects, and reporting to stakeholders. You’ll write code, design architecture, learn customer needs, and deliver groundbreaking product improvements - only the highest bar of quality and positive impact is acceptable at Pryzm.



Qualifications:

Located in or willing to relocate to Boston, MA or New York, NY. US Person due to sensitive data requirements. Experience with full-stack web development, cloud deployment infrastructure, and data pipelines preferred. Veterans strongly encouraged to apply. Willingness to hustle, work hard, and make a dent in the universe.



Benefits:

Pryzm also offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including: Platinum Healthcare: we offer elite medical, dental, and vision coverage that are 100% covered by Pryzm for you and your dependents. Basic Life/AD&D and long-term disability insurance is also 100% covered by Pryzm, with the option to purchase additional life insurance for you and your dependents. Relocation assistance available.

source_jobs.title: Growth (Commercial)
source_jobs.description:
About Pryzm

Pryzm is a startup revolutionizing government contracting, reshaping the way that companies and governments work together. Founded by alumni of top Defense and Artificial Intelligence firms (including Palantir and Lockheed Martin), backed by preeminent investors, and deployed with leading defense companies, join us in our mission to enable our government with the best technology and services possible!



Who we are looking for:

You are a self-starter, ready and able to get things done, and relentless in your pursuit of the mission - nothing will stop you. Intelligent, curious, technically strong, collaborative, and driven. Comfortable operating in ambiguous environments. A maniacal sense of urgency is your operating principle.



What you'll do:

You’ll drive sales from lead generation to close, partnering closely with our government customers to accomplish their goals. Identify leads, travel to conferences and industry days, shape opportunities, and successfully win contracts. And from there, do everything you can to ensure our customers are successful in accomplishing their goals with Pryzm.



Qualifications:

Located in or willing to relocate to Boston, MA or Washington, DC. US Person. Preference for previous government acquisition or sales experience. Willingness to travel. Veterans strongly encouraged to apply. Willingness to hustle, work hard, and make a dent in the universe.



Benefits:

Pryzm also offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including: Platinum Healthcare: we offer elite medical, dental, and vision coverage that are 100% covered by Pryzm for you and your dependents. Basic Life/AD&D and long-term disability insurance is also 100% covered by Pryzm, with the option to purchase additional life insurance for you and your dependents. Relocation assistance available.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Pryzm

America wastes time and money when the right capabilities don’t reach the right missions fast enough. Pryzm is fixing that. We’re building the AI operating system for federal procurement and capture -- used by both government and industry -- to unify workflows, market and budget intelligence, human context, and real-time program awareness into one actionable picture. Our team comes from Palantir, Lockheed Martin, and high-tempo startups; we ship secure, reliable software that moves real programs forward.

If you want your code to matter to national security outcomes, join us.



The Role

I’m hiring a Software Engineering Lead to help me (the CTO) improve how federal tech gets delivered.

This is a hands-on leadership role: you’ll own critical systems end-to-end, set a high technical bar, and grow a team that ships with speed and rigor. We operate where it’s hardest -- security, reliability, scale, and clarity under ambiguity.

This role is ideal for a senior level individual contributor with demonstrated experience conducting code-reviews, mentoring junior developers and liaising across other business units.



What You’ll Do

 - Lead the design and development of complex, full-stack software systems, contributing hands-on across the stack.

 - Review code, maintain high engineering standards, and mentor engineers through technical guidance and feedback.
   Scope and design scalable system architectures, ensuring reliability, performance, and maintainability.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with teams such as Sales, Mission Ops, and Marketing to align product delivery with customer and business needs.

 - Drive development workflows and release coordination, ensuring smooth deployments and high-quality production systems.

 - Contribute to building modern AI-enabled products, including backend services, workflows, and integrations with LLM-powered systems.

 - Help grow the engineering team by participating in hiring and technical interviews.
   
   

What We're Looking For

 - Led teams building or personally built complex, distributed systems with real users and uptime requirements.

 - Demonstrated mastery in at least one of our core areas:
   
   - Backend/Data: APIs, ETL
   
   - Frontend: UI/UX
   
   - Search/Analytics: PostgreSQL, Elasticsearch, schema design, relevance tuning.

 - Designed resilient architectures: clear boundaries, smooth releases, and instrumentation that catches issues before users do.

 - Communicated clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders. You can argue from first principles and then ship the pragmatic thing..
   
   

Qualifications

 - 5+ years of professional software engineering experience with demonstrated technical leadership on complex systems or products.

 - A hands-on engineer who still enjoys coding while taking on leadership responsibilities.

 - Background as a Tech Lead, Senior/Staff Engineer, early engineer at a high-growth startup, or founder/co-founder of a startup.

 - Experience building modern web applications with frameworks such as React or Next.js, with strong product intuition and UX/design sensibility.

 - Backend expertise with modern frameworks such as Django, FastAPI, or Node.js, along with strong SQL and system design fundamentals.

 - Experience building or integrating AI/LLM-powered systems or workflows

 - Familiarity with modern infrastructure and tooling such as AWS, GCP, or Azure, along with workflow or systems tooling (e.g., Temporal, Airflow, Prefect, Redis, OAuth).

 - Bonus experience with distributed systems, ETL/data pipelines, DevOps tooling (Kubernetes, Helm), or mobile development.

 - Strong communicator who is comfortable collaborating with cross-functional teams and occasionally engaging with customers.

 - Motivated, intellectually curious, and driven to build exceptional products with a high-performing team.

 - Located in or willing to relocate to Boston, MA.

 - US Person.

 - Veterans strongly encouraged to ap

source_jobs.title: Head of Marketing
source_jobs.description:
About Pryzm

Pryzm is transforming how companies engage with the federal government.

Today, federal procurement and capture is fragmented across disconnected workflows, siloed intelligence, and institutional knowledge locked in spreadsheets and inboxes. Pryzm brings together market intelligence, budget data, program visibility, and human insights into a single platform that helps teams navigate and win in the federal ecosystem.

Built by alumni from Palantir, Lockheed Martin, and leading AI companies, Pryzm is trusted by organizations across the national security and federal technology ecosystem. Our mission is simple: deliver the right capabilities to the missions that matter most.




THE ROLE

As Pryzm’s Head of Marketing you will define Pryzm’s brand strategy.

This is a high-impact, founding-level opportunity for someone who wants to define how Pryzm presents itself to the world and shape the marketing voice of a rapidly scaling defense and federal civilian technology company.

You will work closely with leadership, product, engineering, and sales to translate technical capabilities into clear, compelling messaging that resonates with our customers.

You will play a foundational role in shaping Pryzm’s brand, launches, and marketing engine as the company scales.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Own Pryzm’s product narrative and positioning

 - Define and continuously refine how Pryzm communicates its value to the federal and national security ecosystem.

 - Translate complex product capabilities into clear, compelling messaging that resonates with customers and differentiates Pryzm in the market.

Lead product launches and go-to-market execution

 - Partner with product and engineering to plan and execute product launches, ensuring new capabilities are introduced with strong messaging, coordinated campaigns, and clear customer value.

Enable the sales team with best-in-class collateral

 - Develop high-quality sales assets, including pitch decks, one-pagers, case studies, and demo narratives, that help the sales team clearly communicate Pryzm’s value and win complex enterprise deals.

Shape Pryzm’s digital marketing presence

 - Own messaging and customer journey across the website and key digital channels, and develop content that drives awareness, engagement, and qualified inbound pipeline.

Build and scale marketing foundations

 - Work with leadership and external partners on communications, PR, and marketing initiatives while helping establish the early marketing infrastructure that will support Pryzm’s growth.
   
   

What We’re Looking For

 - Deep gig experience in owning and developing visuals, content, brands, and narratives, or 2+ years of VC-backed, high growth SaaS marketing experience (at least 1 year of product marketing experience)

 - Background in determining strategic direction and execute against that strategy

 - Experience translating complex products into clear, compelling messaging

 - Exceptional taste and design preferences as evidenced by your portfolio

 - Familiarity with digital marketing, content strategy, and brand positioning

 - Comfort operating in a fast-moving startup environment with significant ownership

 - Strong product intuition and the ability to collaborate closely with engineers and product teams
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Located in or willing to relocate to Boston, DC, or NY

 - US Person

 - Veterans strongly encouraged to apply

 - Willingness for occasional travel
   


BENEFITS

Pryzm offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including:

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Platinum healthcare: comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage fully covered for employees and dependents

 - Basic Life/AD&D and long-term disability insurance fully covered, with the option to purchase additional life insurance for you and your dependents.

 - Relocation assistance available

source_jobs.title: Finance
source_jobs.description:
Position: Finance & Revenue Operations

Location: Boston (preferred, On-Site), NYC (On-Site), Washington DC (On-Site)



About Pryzm

Pryzm is transforming how companies engage with the federal government. Today, federal procurement and BD is fragmented across disconnected workflows, siloed intelligence, and institutional knowledge locked in spreadsheets and inboxes. Pryzm brings together market intelligence, budget data, program visibility, and human insights into a single platform that helps teams navigate and win in the federal ecosystem. Built by alumni from Palantir, Lockheed Martin, and leading AI companies, Pryzm is backed by Tier 1 investors including a16z, and is trusted by organizations across the national security and federal technology ecosystem. Our mission is simple: deliver the best capabilities to the missions that matter most.



The Role

We’re looking for our first dedicated finance hire, to own both strategic finance and core accounting and enable our continued growth. You’ll partner with our Chief of Staff, CGO, and CEO in a high-impact hands-on role as you help build the finance infrastructure and prepare us for continued growth. You’ll work directly with the leadership team to manage cash, support strategic decisions, and ensure the company operates with financial clarity and discipline. You’ll also establish the core accounting and compliance infrastructure needed to scale.



What You’ll Do

 - Own the company financial modeling, including forecasting, budgeting, and scenario planning.

 - Create a strong startup finance reporting cadence, reviewing growth, retention, etc.

 - Help define how finance works for an AI-native company, including modeling inference costs, infrastructure efficiency, and next-generation unit economics.

 - Manage cash flow, runway, and support fundraising efforts

 - Build and maintain clear reporting for leadership, investors, and the board

 - Establish and run core accounting processes (monthly close, financial statements, audits)

 - Oversee payroll, AP/AR, tax filings, and external accounting partners

 - Implement financial controls and ensure compliance as we scale

 - Develop key metrics and dashboards to track business performance

 - Act as the primary finance partner to GTM, Engineering, and Operations leaders

 - Partner with GTM to build and optimize revenue operations, including pipeline tracking, forecasting accuracy, and sales efficiency metrics

 - Negotiate with vendors to get us preferred rates on various costs.



What We’re Looking For

 - Must have 2+ years of experience (ideally 3+) in SaaS / AI-type business, to understand the nature of software accounting.

 - Bonus if spent time at Seed-Series B stage startup

 - Built and owned financial models used for real business decisions (e.g., fundraising, pricing, or forecasting)

 - Experience managing or overseeing accounting operations

 - Ability to operate independently and build systems from scratch

 - Comfort operating in a fast-moving startup environment with significant ownership

 - Clear communicator who can translate financial insights into business decisions

 - Located in or willing to relocate to Boston (preferred), DC, or NY. This is a fully on-site position.



Why Join Pryzm:

 - Opportunity to Build: You’ll be our first dedicated finance hire, defining how finance operates at Pryzm from core accounting infrastructure to strategic decision-making.

 - Strong traction: Strong PMF with leaders in the National Security ecosystem, and Tier 1 Investors

 - Work directly with founders: You’ll partner closely with the CEO and Chief of Staff on the most important decisions in the business, with high ownership and trajectory

 - Shape an AI-native company: You’ll help define financial systems and metrics for a new category, including modeling inference costs, infrastructure efficiency, and next-generation unit economics.

 - Mission-driven impact: Pryzm supports organizations working on national se

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Position: Deployment Strategist

Location: Boston, NYC, Washington DC (On-Site)



About Pryzm

Pryzm is transforming how organizations engage with the federal government. Today, federal procurement and business development is fragmented across disconnected workflows, siloed intelligence, and institutional knowledge locked in spreadsheets and inboxes. Pryzm’s AI brings together market intelligence, budget data, program visibility, and human insights into a single platform that helps teams navigate and win in the federal ecosystem. Big data, meets CRM, meets AI. Our mission is simple: deliver the right capabilities to the missions that matter most.



The Role

Deployment Strategists are the tip of the spear at Pryzm: the people who sit at the intersection of software, strategy, and mission. This is not a traditional implementation role. You will embed directly with our customers in the national security and federal technology space, learn their hardest problems, and build the workflows, analyses, and operational capabilities that transform how they work.



You will work alongside engineers and GTM teams to shape the roadmap, represent the customer's voice inside Pryzm, and ensure that our platform delivers measurable impact in the field. We are looking for people who thrive in ambiguity, build trust with operators and executives alike, and are driven by outcomes.



What You’ll Do

 - Embed with customers to understand their workflows, priorities, and decision-making processes from the inside out

 - Design and deploy tailored workflows and analytical tools using the Pryzm platform to solve real operational problems

 - Translate ambiguous mission requirements into clear product configurations, data integrations, and actionable insights

 - Build and maintain trusted relationships with end users, program managers, and senior leaders at customer organizations

 - Partner with Pryzm’s engineering teams to communicate what’s working, what’s missing, and what matters most

 - Drive adoption and capability expansion within existing accounts, identifying new use cases and stakeholders

 - Develop training materials, playbooks, and documentation that scale your impact beyond direct engagement

 - Support business development by participating in demos, capability briefings, and proposal efforts



What We’re Looking For

 - 2–5 years of experience in a high-impact, customer-facing role, either or already as a Deployment Strategist or Forward Deployed Engineer, or elsewhere, such as within consulting, federal contracting, SaaS implementation, or a hybrid.

 - Demonstrated ability to work directly with operators and decision-makers in government or national security environments

 - Strong analytical instincts: comfortable with data, able to structure complex problems, and fast at learning new tools

 - A builder’s mindset: you don’t wait to be told what to do; you identify the gap and close it

 - Outstanding communication skills with an ability to translate technical concepts for a variety of audiences.

 - Comfort with ambiguity and a bias toward action in fast-moving, high-stakes environments

 - Experience with government procurement, capture management, or the federal technology ecosystem is a strong plus.

 - Bonus: if you are technical and can write code too!



Qualifications

 - Located in or willing to relocate to New York, Washington DC, or Boston. This is a fully on-site position.

 - US Person required

 - Veterans and those with military or intelligence community backgrounds strongly encouraged to apply

 - Willingness for regular customer travel



Benefits

Pryzm offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including:

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Platinum healthcare: comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage fully covered for employees and dependents

 - Basic Life/AD&D and long-term disability insurance fully covered, with the option to purchase additional life insurance for you and y

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Position: Forward Deployed Engineer (Delta)

Location: Boston, NYC, Washington DC (On-Site)



About Pryzm

Pryzm is transforming how organizations engage with the federal government. Today, federal procurement and business development is fragmented across disconnected workflows, siloed intelligence, and institutional knowledge locked in spreadsheets and inboxes. Pryzm’s AI brings together market intelligence, budget data, program visibility, and human insights into a single platform that helps teams navigate and win in the federal ecosystem. Big data, meets CRM, meets AI. Our mission is simple: deliver the right capabilities to the missions that matter most.



The Role

Forward Deployed Engineers at Pryzm are the rare engineers who are equally at home in a terminal and in a customer briefing. You will live at the boundary between our product and the hardest operational problems in the federal ecosystem: understanding user needs, implementing product, connecting integrations, and writing code to deliver on the most important outcomes for a customer - whether a government contractor or the government themselves.



FDEs are builders who also sit close to the customer, accelerating time-to-value for new deployments, and solving problems that no one has fully defined yet. We are looking for product-minded engineers who are energized by constraints, thrive under ambiguity, and find deep satisfaction in seeing their work create real-world impact, not just ship.



What You’ll Do

 - Deploy and configure the Pryzm platform in customer environments, including data ingestion pipelines, integrations, and custom workflow automation

 - Write production-quality code (scripts, APIs, and lightweight applications) to extend platform capabilities and solve customer-specific problems

 - Integrate Pryzm with third-party data sources, government systems, and customer infrastructure

 - Diagnose and resolve technical issues rapidly in the field, with limited access to core infrastructure or remote support

 - Translate operational requirements from non-technical stakeholders into clean technical specifications and working software

 - Build tooling, templates, and reusable components that accelerate future deployments

 - Partner with the product engineering team to surface field-discovered bugs, feature gaps, and architecture feedback

 - Own technical relationships with customer engineers, data teams, and IT/security leads

 - Contribute to internal knowledge bases, deployment playbooks, and engineering documentation

What We’re Looking For

 - 2+ years of software engineering / forward deployed engineering experience, with a strong foundation in at least one backend language (Python, Go, Java, or similar), at a top tier-startup, enterprise, or your own projects

 - Hands-on experience deploying or integrating software in complex, real-world environments, not just greenfield development

 - Fluency with data: SQL, ETL pipelines, REST APIs, and working with structured and unstructured data sources

 - Strong debugging instincts and the ability to diagnose problems across the full stack, including infrastructure and networking layers

 - A customer mindset: you understand that the best technical solution is the one that actually gets used

 - Exceptional written and verbal communication: you can explain a complex system to a program manager and a junior analyst in the same conversation

 - Experience working in or alongside the federal government, defense, or intelligence community is a strong plus



Qualifications

 - Located in or willing to relocate to New York, Washington DC, or Boston. This is a fully on-site position.

 - US Person required

 - Veterans and those with military or intelligence community backgrounds strongly encouraged to apply

 - Willingness for regular customer travel



Benefits

Pryzm offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including:

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Platinum healthcare: com

source_jobs.title: Scaled Customer Success
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PRYZM

Pryzm is transforming how organizations engage with the federal government.

Today, federal procurement and business development are fragmented across disconnected workflows, siloed intelligence, and institutional knowledge trapped in spreadsheets and inboxes. Pryzm brings together market intelligence, budget data, program visibility, and human insights into a single AI-powered platform that helps teams navigate and win in the federal ecosystem.

Big data meets CRM meets AI.

Our mission is simple: deliver the right capabilities to the missions that matter most.




THE ROLE

We're looking for an early Customer Success leader to help build and scale Pryzm's post-sale organization.

This is not a traditional CSM role. You won't simply manage accounts or run check-ins. You'll own the systems, processes, and customer programs that ensure customers successfully onboard, adopt, renew, and expand their use of Pryzm.

You'll work directly with customers while also building the operational foundation that allows Pryzm to serve a growing customer base efficiently. That means designing onboarding experiences, creating customer education programs, establishing customer health processes, improving support operations, and identifying opportunities to strengthen retention and expansion across the portfolio.

You'll partner closely with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Implementation, serving as both the voice of the customer and a builder of the systems that scale customer success.

If you're energized by creating structure where none exists, using technology to solve operational problems, and helping customers realize meaningful value from software, we'd love to meet you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


CUSTOMER SUCCESS & RETENTION

 - Monitor customer health across the customer base and identify leading indicators of risk and expansion.

 - Build proactive processes for churn prevention, adoption, and renewal readiness.

 - Partner with Sales to surface and develop expansion opportunities.

 - Serve as a strategic advisor to customers, ensuring they achieve measurable outcomes with Pryzm.


SUPPORT OPERATIONS

 - Own and improve customer support workflows, ticketing processes, and escalation paths.

 - Ensure customer issues are triaged, tracked, and resolved effectively.

 - Build reporting and dashboards to measure support performance and customer health.

 - Coordinate closely with Product and Engineering to resolve customer challenges.


ONBOARDING & ENABLEMENT

 - Design and run scalable onboarding programs for new customers.

 - Create playbooks, documentation, training materials, webinars, and customer education resources.

 - Continuously improve the Pryzm User Guide and customer-facing content library.

 - Measure onboarding effectiveness and reduce time-to-value for new users.


SYSTEMS & PROCESS BUILDING

 - Design repeatable customer success processes that scale as the business grows.

 - Leverage AI, automation, and software systems to improve efficiency and customer outcomes.

 - Identify operational gaps and build solutions proactively.

 - Establish best practices for how Pryzm engages customers after the sale.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2–6 years of experience in Customer Success, Account Management, Solutions Consulting, Operations, or another customer-facing SaaS role.

 - Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

 - Demonstrated ability to manage customer relationships while also improving processes and systems behind the scenes.

 - Excellent organizational skills and a track record of owning complex initiatives from start to finish.

 - Strong commercial instincts with an understanding of customer health, retention, and expansion.

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and building without a playbook.

 - A bias toward action and a willingness to take ownership of problems. High-urgency a must.

 - Tech-native and systems-oriented; naturally looks for opportunities to automate and scale.

 - Experi

notes: Pryzm is a startup with a strong engineering focus, transforming government contracting with AI-powered solutions.
